[](https://substack..DxkMZolcHR_P_XpVjrLOsxBnLXwP1NaPjHe7PsaGkpY?)
---
# [The GOP capitalizes on ethics failures by Democrats](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=98994398&utm_source=post-email-title&isFreemail=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6OTg5OTQzOTgsImlhdCI6MTY3NDc1MzA5MywiZXhwIjoxNjc3MzQ1MDkzLCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMTAxODU5NyIsInN1YiI6InBvc3QtcmVhY3Rpb24ifQ.z9HthxMTOlp7pQXEPjUx5KCOwbuYyHDhp-MQ_LzT-yE)
### Democrats--and newspaper editors--created preventable opportunities for right wing Republicans by embracing congressional conflicts of interest
| | [Shahid Buttar](https://substack.com/redirect/81c5ced6-2cad-4b8f-b89b-0540612602a0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
---
| Jan 26| | | ∙| | Preview
---|---|---
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/81c5ced6-2cad-4b8f-b89b-0540612602a0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
---
---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=98994398&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6OTg5OTQzOTgsInJlYWN0aW9uIjoi4p2kIiwiaWF0IjoxNjc0NzUzMDkzLCJleHAiOjE2NzczNDUwOTMsImlzcyI6InB1Yi0xMDE4NTk3Iiwic3ViIjoicmVhY3Rpb24ifQ.R4FNper1RsDRBsbVGQwS1PLfanBErZvn4w-6BAt0reg&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=98994398&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6OTg5OTQzOTgsImlhdCI6MTY3NDc1MzA5MywiZXhwIjoxNjc3MzQ1MDkzLCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMTAxODU5NyIsInN1YiI6InBvc3QtcmVhY3Rpb24ifQ.z9HthxMTOlp7pQXEPjUx5KCOwbuYyHDhp-MQ_LzT-yE&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=98994398&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6OTg5OTQzOTgsImlhdCI6MTY3NDc1MzA5MywiZXhwIjoxNjc3MzQ1MDkzLCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMTAxODU5NyIsInN1YiI6InBvc3QtcmVhY3Rpb24ifQ.z9HthxMTOlp7pQXEPjUx5KCOwbuYyHDhp-MQ_LzT-yE)
---
| | [Save](https://substack..EvQCDjJ5gODVxahKsAoDcu883KMDL43LjkvjDjZqIfM?)
---
[This post is also accessible as [a podcast](https://substack.com/redirect/9f6f2a71-8cab-45b7-8bee-e4d88332fc3b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)]
This week, Senator Josh Hawley (R-MO) [introduced](https://substack.com/redirect/6341dfc1-462e-4b3a-b939-3e184c2f3c13?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) an important piece of [legislation](https://substack.com/redirect/8a23ec63-0eea-41fe-8ed8-e9db0fafdc13?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) aimed to address the bipartisan conflicts of interest that pervade Congress. While his proposal to prohibit insider trading by policymakers is plagued by crucial deficiencies, it remains important for reasons that are both constitutional and political.
Hawley's bill is called the Preventing Elected Leaders from Owning Securities and Investments Act, the PELOSI Act. It trolls Democrats by naming the former Speaker as a literal poster child for corruption, while appealing to a faux populism and claiming to care about ethics that Hawley & his colleagues have abandoned in the past. Republicans, after all, have historically promoted Wall Street's interests even more aggressively than Democrats. And Hawley himself is so committed to democracy that he [invited](https://substack.com/redirect/54939951-f23c-4e72-ac8c-a2dbd4684c5c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) an attempted insurrection from which he was seemingly poised to benefit.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/0f09739b-7c28-4ba5-b36c-05db64a1473f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Ultimately, however, policy is more important than personality--and ethics more important than ideology.
Having spent the last six years calling out Nancy Pelosi's track record on precisely this issue, I'm grateful to see this proposal emerge, regardless of the source. I'm frankly compelled to observe the absurdity of this saga after watching media outlets join Democrats, from "grassroots" political clubs to elected officials, who conspired in 2020 to insulate Pelosi & her corruption rather than stand by (any of) their own stated principles.
### **Recalling the Constitution 's Emoluments Clause**
The Constitution includes [a provision](https://substack.com/redirect/9a92bd1e-b829-4ee4-a879-ffd0b029e250?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that explicitly prohibits public officials from enriching themselves through public office. Allowing policymakers to trade stocks, in contrast, effectively invites them to place their private portfolios before the public interest. Politicians from (former Republican Speaker of the House) [John Boehner](https://substack.com/redirect/a153c87c-970f-477e-8ac7-960dc9b556d9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to (former Democratic Speaker of the House) Nancy Pelosi have done precisely that throughout their long careers.
[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=98994398&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6OTg5OTQzOTgsImlhdCI6MTY3NDc1MzA5MywiZXhwIjoxNjc3MzQ1MDkzLCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMTAxODU5NyIsInN1YiI6InBvc3QtcmVhY3Rpb24ifQ.z9HthxMTOlp7pQXEPjUx5KCOwbuYyHDhp-MQ_LzT-yE)
Crucially, the Emoluments Clause was [the key to removing Donald Trump from the presidency](https://substack.com/redirect/73522494-aada-463d-b025-b782176408c4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) during the theatrical, half-hearted impeachment charades that Pelosi eventually supported in 2019 and 2020, after years of impeding executive accountability for presidential administrations [ever since](https://substack.com/redirect/a2e65d76-658e-419d-aea4-bd1572fe6e79?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) the Bush administration.
Half a year before the first impeachment, I [wrote](https://substack.com/redirect/73522494-aada-463d-b025-b782176408c4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) on Truthout in May 2019:
> We need leaders in Congress who are ready and willing to rise to the occasion by checking and balancing the executive branch. Failing to impeach will only embolden our kleptocrat-in-chief.
> Many supporters of impeachment proceedings have suggested that the House begin the process immediately. The potential grounds for impeachment seem endless: the president has obstructed justice, colluded with a foreign power, violated the emoluments clause by enriching himself through office, and made a litany of documented lies to the public, the press and policymakers themselves.
> But Democratic Party leaders, including House Speaker Nancy Pelosi, have insisted that impeachment proceedings are not a priority. While impeachment is still technically on the table, Pelosi claims that Trump "just isn't worth it."
Pelosi, of course, did eventually show up for impeachment--like a boxer throwing a fight.
Even after finally conceding and accepting that impeachment was inevitable, Pelosi declined to accept the [recommendation](https://substack.com/redirect/d11e072a-1bcb-4ae9-a9da-1cfd4142f582?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of Rep. Jamie Raskin (D-MD) to [include](https://substack.com/redirect/88444764-ebf9-4f54-8790-6c7465542d54?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) charges based on Emoluments violations within the Articles of Impeachment. That reduced the impeachment proceedings in the Senate to largely partisan affairs. A focus on the former president's self-enrichment at the public's expense could have shined a light on bipartisan problems and unified a divided public behind ethics principles that remain, years later, in exile.
[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=98994398&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6OTg5OTQzOTgsImlhdCI6MTY3NDc1MzA5MywiZXhwIjoxNjc3MzQ1MDkzLCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMTAxODU5NyIsInN1YiI6InBvc3QtcmVhY3Rpb24ifQ.z9HthxMTOlp7pQXEPjUx5KCOwbuYyHDhp-MQ_LzT-yE)
To what extent was Pelosi's reticence to explore Trump's corruption driven by her own dirty laundry? The strongest reason to water down the impeachment charges appeared to be because self-enrichment is such a thoroughly bipartisan practice in Washington that Democrats would have made themselves vulnerable to GOP claims of hypocrisy and selective enforcement had they proceeded.
Our nation continues to pay the price for Democrats who lacked the independence to do their constitutional duty, either when a criminal president inhabited the White House from 2016-2020, or over the decades since their colleagues started putting their private fortunes before human rights and the needs of working Americans.
### **Creating an attack surface for the GOP**
The performance of Pelosi's private stock portfolio consistently outpaces that of Wall Street investment professionals, so much so that [some portfolios](https://substack.com/redirect/2cc666fd-0a4b-4d93-a3bf-c265bba7a13b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) aim explicitly to mirror her holdings.
What explains the most powerful Democrat in Congress achieving the [best investment returns](https://substack.com/redirect/a7114327-ed19-4727-ba87-ba24c7e7149e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of any policymaker in Washington, let alone beating Wall Street professionals? Her statistically inexplicable success indicates that the inside information to which she uniquely has access has influenced her investment decisions. To presume otherwise requires abandoning reason.
It is equally ridiculous to bend over backwards in imaging that Pelosi's ownership interests did not influence her work as the most powerful policymaker in Washington.
[Pelosi infamously blocked legislation to protect consumers](https://substack.com/redirect/4234ffbd-c5d0-4db4-9c56-1b8f49c983e8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) after purchasing stock in the initial public offering for Visa in 2008. She later profited handsomely from ownership positions in companies from [Google](https://substack.com/redirect/fbf73427-0566-4c86-897b-b401fda01f33?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to Tesla, and many others, before public announcements about government decisions impacting those companies.
For instance, Pelosi sold Google stock worth millions of dollars in December 2022, one month before the federal Department of Justice and eight states' Attorneys General announced an antitrust suit earlier this week seeking accountability for Google's anticompetitive practices leveraging monopoly power over a "wide swath" of online advertising tools while ultimately corrupting "legitimate competition in the ad tech industry." [Newsweek observed](https://substack.com/redirect/fbf73427-0566-4c86-897b-b401fda01f33?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) the curious serendipity of Pelosi's timing.
Pelosi noted her personal commitment to capitalism in [response to a question from an NYU student in 2017](https://substack.com/redirect/55ffb765-1fed-46f7-8159-37bc43f37f37?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Left unspecified was whether the "we" to whom she referred was the U.S. Congress or instead herself and her increasingly wealthy family.
While technically unrelated to insider trading, Pelosi has also violated the Emoluments Clause in other ways, particularly by benefiting from government programs crafted to help those in need. When the COVID-19 pandemic forced small business owners around the country into dire straits, Congress approved a [Paycheck Protection Program](https://substack.com/redirect/24362788-4e45-4440-8c25-b5f1bffcf9b7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to help offset the costs of payroll. Many of the bay area's most [desperate](https://substack.com/redirect/8af4818b-b570-44e4-957a-87c8e89b99ff?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) entrepreneurs and [diverse communities never saw](https://substack.com/redirect/954adf1a-b124-4b44-8b36-53f6082a7cb7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) those funds, but one business that did receive them was [owned in part](https://substack.com/redirect/04937705-a3ca-4f45-a64b-863b3caff285?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) by Pelosi's husband.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..1oOhFlXmGjO7nOfxqyBiSu_tHgLmRF-c3EiiU5ngN8s?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=98994398)
### **Unpeeling layers of corruption**
#### _The market_
Even after long overdue reports about congressional insider trading finally emerged in the past year, most of the critique came from [voices](https://substack.com/redirect/421840fb-e497-411d-9813-aad470d3a6fc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) concerned about the co-optation of financial markets by oligarchs using inside information to take advantage of other investors who don't enjoy the same access.
The law has [long prohibited](https://substack.com/redirect/a73176f1-79c3-4bd7-bf2f-066382cb7ea7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) corporate executives from making trades based on information not accessible to the public. That has been illegal for decades.
The obvious question is: why are policymakers exempt from a rule that already constrains executives? After all, executives are poised to wield inside information only about a particular enterprise, whereas policymakers have access to inside information affecting each and every one of them.
That obvious question has an equally obvious answer: Congress writes the laws, and nothing constrains the insider trading millionaires who dominate it from contriving the law to suit their craven, self-serving purposes.
But even all that is just the tip of the iceberg. Far more destructive than the corruption of investment markets is the corruption of the federal policymaking process.
#### _Public policy_
Corrupting Wall Street means that small investors get fleeced by richer ones, like the insider trading millionaires who dominate Congress. That's bad for lots of people, from workers investing in 401k funds to retirees or people living on fixed incomes, who invest in stocks as part of a long-term plan for financial security, only to be preyed upon by powerful people using their elected offices as a lever to fill their wallets.
But corrupting Congress is worse, both because it harms everyone (not only investors), and also because it neutralizes the source of potential support best poised to alleviate the vagaries of the market: democratically accountable government.
The conflicts of interest across Congress hamstring public responses to corporate predation. They predictably encourage indefensible public policy outcomes--like, for instance, recurring [tax cuts for millionaires](https://substack.com/redirect/084ed1e7-c31e-4a2a-99ac-c58dbe08dfaa?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) alongside a federal minimum wage that has remained [frozen](https://substack.com/redirect/7147ca67-861f-4781-81e1-cdbd266d01cf?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) for well over a decade, or repeated approvals for [runaway](https://substack.com/redirect/0004df5f-446f-4874-b76f-cc308698cdb1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) military spending that inflate the profits of [corporate contractors](https://substack.com/redirect/e0b9dc9c-ab2b-4d2a-95de-603cc3633106?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in which [many policymakers have invested](https://substack.com/redirect/8a7c5ec7-0584-40ac-9d1d-44240d4c1b1d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
This pattern is a crucial part of why President [Eisenhower warned our country in 1961](https://substack.com/redirect/35a12d01-b14e-4809-a38c-9c575552168b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to fear the machinations of a military-industrial complex that has only tightened its grip around America's throat in the years since then.
#### _Elected officials_
Members of Congress who are more interested in capitalism than democracy are free to build their fortunes through private enterprise. Those who subject the machinery of government to the enlargement of their own fortunes display a disqualifying disregard for democracy by openly embracing conflicts of interest.
Every one of them--including the senior leadership of both corporate parties in Washington--stands in open violation of a constitutional prohibition, despite swearing an oath to uphold that document and the principles it embodies.
#### _Editors & journalists_
As much as policymakers deserve criticism for failing to regulate themselves, another community deserves even greater blame for falling asleep at the switch: the newspaper editors whose primary responsibility was to expose corruption but instead chose to embrace it in plain sight.
Congressional insider trading has never been OK. It's absurd that [it took until 2022](https://substack.com/redirect/1a93caf5-93d3-4e45-8d8d-acf7ba05ba06?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) for the press to notice an obvious vector inviting conflicts of interest, and that even in 2023, few voices have acknowledged the profound institutional corruption resulting from it. It's equally absurd that this issue has fallen to the GOP only because editors have so pervasively failed to ethically cover political news in real-time.
### **Observing the impacts of institutional media failure**
Hawley's proposal is neither the first to curtail congressional conflicts of interest, nor the most demanding.
Two years before he introduced the PELOSI Act to curtail insider trading by policymakers, I [proposed](https://substack.com/redirect/568fe622-ac87-4058-87c8-7c154c289dab?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) ending the practice through a more aggressive solution that most voters never heard about.
My proposal would have reached not only policymakers and their spouses, as Hawley's would, but also their children, extended family, and staff. No policy that leaves congressional family members or staff free to leverage inside information would suffice to stop conflicts of interest from subverting the policymaking process.
Unlike Sen. Hawley, I also proposed to prohibit policymakers, their families and their staff from trading not only stocks and other financial instruments, but also from investing in private equity vehicles that are even more [insidiously](https://substack.com/redirect/987c50e8-39a1-4712-8c92-d788b792b02f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) [opaque](https://substack.com/redirect/d55a62b3-9d6e-4162-a1be-d5ee70d857d1?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and unaccountable. Private equity has wrought remarkable havoc on [housing](https://substack.com/redirect/0f3e57b0-fa13-4c17-a5a1-619b3a3312b3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) markets, [healthcare](https://substack.com/redirect/cc42cf7c-8118-48be-88c9-bafac9a5af49?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) (even according to Wall Street [apologists](https://substack.com/redirect/f9761d00-c72f-4ded-bb1f-14c55711534f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)) and [public health](https://substack.com/redirect/9d144a9f-0fdc-47ea-a841-fc12d6772ce9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), as well as [journalism](https://substack.com/redirect/d9d7a228-a793-4c4b-b688-0afb881994bc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) (coincidence?), and indeed, [our economy](https://substack.com/redirect/18862d34-46da-4755-96ca-38b9a028c327?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) writ large.
Any so-called "reform" that would leave in place opportunities for policymakers to launder their conflicts of interest by simply making them less visible--as Hawley's would--is more cosmetic than meaningful.
We produced this advertisement supporting my 2022 congressional campaign to highlight the issue and summarize my proposal:
[Shahid Buttar (find me on Substack) @ShahidForChangeCongressional insider trading is an open invitation to corruption. It entrenches corporate influence, ensuring that Congress serves Wall Street before the rest of us. Pelosi continues ducking debates. RT to help expose the contrasts between our city's options on the ballot! ](https://substack.com/redirect/568fe622-ac87-4058-87c8-7c154c289dab?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)[10:50 PM ∙ May 20, 2022
* * *
277Likes117Retweets](https://substack.com/redirect/568fe622-ac87-4058-87c8-7c154c289dab?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Unlike most congressional races, our's did not suffer from a dearth of press coverage. Whether because we raised over $1.6 million, or instead because my voice attracted support from every state across the entire country and every county in California, [many press outlets](https://substack.com/redirect/48ff6c6e-bcfb-45f8-8bde-f037cbee1f2e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) around the country--from Rolling Stone and Salon to The Intercept and the Los Angeles Times--found our race newsworthy.
Yet, in spite of all the attention, most voters never heard about my ideas or policy proposals. First, nearly every local broadcast outlet ignored us. Only two English-language local media covered us at all. KPFA, the local Pacifica Radio affiliate, covered our campaign the morning after I won the primary. And KTVU, the local Fox affiliate, posted [a story and video segment](https://substack.com/redirect/b9bf3632-462b-4cc3-b4e1-fad37bd409dc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in the waning weeks of the election. But aside from viewers of those two specific broadcasts, most San Franciscans had no idea that Pelosi was even up for re-election in 2020--let alone how the candidates differed, or that the November 2020 ballot included our city's first contested congressional election since 1987.
But even among [the outlets that did cover my campaign](https://substack.com/redirect/0715f478-4dc7-4ed8-9e8c-74eb37543e99?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), the policies on which I ran--like banning congressional insider trading, or adopting universal healthcare during a global pandemic, or nationalizing the fossil fuel industry that continues to threaten humanity and life on Earth--appeared far less [interesting](https://substack.com/redirect/9e83c97a-12bc-4a2d-964c-f484e23ac175?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to editors than [racist lies](https://substack.com/redirect/661ace30-00df-4818-9687-6f509ca4fb50?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) contrived by Democrats loyal to Pelosi.
The corruption of policymakers enriching themselves, in other words, has been insulated by the corruption of writers and journalists who failed to ethically practice their profession and instead abandoned independence to publish partisan propaganda entrenching corruption.
That's among the reasons why I continue to wage [a lawsuit in federal district court](https://substack.com/redirect/fdbfdc58-03e9-497f-829a-29f84e75cff3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) against _the San Francisco Chronicle_. Beyond recklessly and maliciously publishing racist disinformation fabricated to undermine a-once-in-a-generation-election, journalists from outlets including _The Intercept_ , _Mission Local_ , _48 Hills_ , the _Bay Area Reporter_ , _Current Affairs_ and others all enabled the continuing corruption of an intergenerational oligarch who continues to fleece the public while shamelessly filling her pockets.
(Feel free to mark your calendar and join us for our next hearing before U.S. District Judge Chen, on February 2 around 1:30pm PDT, about which I'll share more details soon)
In the same way that America continues to pay a price for Democrats who can't be bothered to show up for work, we have also been abandoned by newspaper editors whose abdication of independent reporting has [given cover](https://substack.com/redirect/e9eebb31-996f-408f-9c51-271f1dbce9ec?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to corrupt Democrats whose recurring failures in turn create opportunities for corrupt Republicans.
We should all demand better, regardless of our ideology or political preferences. Ethics and corruption, like any constitutional issue, offer the opportunity to unite warring factions of voters around common sense principles.
Stopping policymakers from profiting from their office is the very least we can do to start restoring faith and trust in government.

Democrats--and newspaper editors--created preventable opportunities for right wing Republicans by embracing congressional conflicts of interest | | | Jan 26| | | ∙| | Preview ---|---|--- | | | | | | | | | | ? ] This week, Senator Josh Hawley (R-MO) an important piece of aimed to address the bipartisan conflicts of interest that pervade Congress. While his proposal to prohibit insider trading by policymakers is plagued by crucial deficiencies, it remains important for reasons that are both constitutional and political. Hawley's bill is called the Preventing Elected Leaders from Owning Securities and Investments Act, the PELOSI Act. It trolls Democrats by naming the former Speaker as a literal poster child for corruption, while appealing to a faux populism and claiming to care about ethics that Hawley & his colleagues have abandoned in the past. Republicans, after all, have historically promoted Wall Street's interests even more aggressively than Democrats. And Hawley himself is so committed to democracy that he an attempted insurrection from which he was seemingly poised to benefit. | | ---|---|--- Ultimately, however, policy is more important than personality--and ethics more important than ideology. Having spent the last six years calling out Nancy Pelosi's track record on precisely this issue, I'm grateful to see this proposal emerge, regardless of the source. I'm frankly compelled to observe the absurdity of this saga after watching media outlets join Democrats, from "grassroots" political clubs to elected officials, who conspired in 2020 to insulate Pelosi & her corruption rather than stand by (any of) their own stated principles. Recalling the Constitution 's Emoluments Clause The Constitution includes that explicitly prohibits public officials from enriching themselves through public office. Allowing policymakers to trade stocks, in contrast, effectively invites them to place their private portfolios before the public interest. Politicians from (former Republican Speaker of the House) to (former Democratic Speaker of the House) Nancy Pelosi have done precisely that throughout their long careers. Crucially, the Emoluments Clause was during the theatrical, half-hearted impeachment charades that Pelosi eventually supported in 2019 and 2020, after years of impeding executive accountability for presidential administrations the Bush administration. Half a year before the first impeachment, I on Truthout in May 2019: We need leaders in Congress who are ready and willing to rise to the occasion by checking and balancing the executive branch. Failing to impeach will only embolden our kleptocrat-in-chief. Many supporters of impeachment proceedings have suggested that the House begin the process immediately. The potential grounds for impeachment seem endless: the president has obstructed justice, colluded with a foreign power, violated the emoluments clause by enriching himself through office, and made a litany of documented lies to the public, the press and policymakers themselves. But Democratic Party leaders, including House Speaker Nancy Pelosi, have insisted that impeachment proceedings are not a priority. While impeachment is still technically on the table, Pelosi claims that Trump "just isn't worth it." Pelosi, of course, did eventually show up for impeachment--like a boxer throwing a fight. Even after finally conceding and accepting that impeachment was inevitable, Pelosi declined to accept the of Rep. Jamie Raskin (D-MD) to charges based on Emoluments violations within the Articles of Impeachment. That reduced the impeachment proceedings in the Senate to largely partisan affairs. A focus on the former president's self-enrichment at the public's expense could have shined a light on bipartisan problems and unified a divided public behind ethics principles that remain, years later, in exile. To what extent was Pelosi's reticence to explore Trump's corruption driven by her own dirty laundry? The strongest reason to water down the impeachment charges appeared to be because self-enrichment is such a thoroughly bipartisan practice in Washington that Democrats would have made themselves vulnerable to GOP claims of hypocrisy and selective enforcement had they proceeded. Our nation continues to pay the price for Democrats who lacked the independence to do their constitutional duty, either when a criminal president inhabited the White House from 2016-2020, or over the decades since their colleagues started putting their private fortunes before human rights and the needs of working Americans. Creating an attack surface for the GOP The performance of Pelosi's private stock portfolio consistently outpaces that of Wall Street investment professionals, so much so that aim explicitly to mirror her holdings. What explains the most powerful Democrat in Congress achieving the of any policymaker in Washington, let alone beating Wall Street professionals? Her statistically inexplicable success indicates that the inside information to which she uniquely has access has influenced her investment decisions. To presume otherwise requires abandoning reason. It is equally ridiculous to bend over backwards in imaging that Pelosi's ownership interests did not influence her work as the most powerful policymaker in Washington. after purchasing stock in the initial public offering for Visa in 2008. She later profited handsomely from ownership positions in companies from to Tesla, and many others, before public announcements about government decisions impacting those companies. For instance, Pelosi sold Google stock worth millions of dollars in December 2022, one month before the federal Department of Justice and eight states' Attorneys General announced an antitrust suit earlier this week seeking accountability for Google's anticompetitive practices leveraging monopoly power over a "wide swath" of online advertising tools while ultimately corrupting "legitimate competition in the ad tech industry." the curious serendipity of Pelosi's timing. Pelosi noted her personal commitment to capitalism in . Left unspecified was whether the "we" to whom she referred was the U.S. Congress or instead herself and her increasingly wealthy family. While technically unrelated to insider trading, Pelosi has also violated the Emoluments Clause in other ways, particularly by benefiting from government programs crafted to help those in need. When the COVID-19 pandemic forced small business owners around the country into dire straits, Congress approved a to help offset the costs of payroll. Many of the bay area's most entrepreneurs and those funds, but one business that did receive them was by Pelosi's husband. Unpeeling layers of corruption The market Even after long overdue reports about congressional insider trading finally emerged in the past year, most of the critique came from concerned about the co-optation of financial markets by oligarchs using inside information to take advantage of other investors who don't enjoy the same access. The law has corporate executives from making trades based on information not accessible to the public. That has been illegal for decades. The obvious question is: why are policymakers exempt from a rule that already constrains executives? After all, executives are poised to wield inside information only about a particular enterprise, whereas policymakers have access to inside information affecting each and every one of them. That obvious question has an equally obvious answer: Congress writes the laws, and nothing constrains the insider trading millionaires who dominate it from contriving the law to suit their craven, self-serving purposes. But even all that is just the tip of the iceberg. Far more destructive than the corruption of investment markets is the corruption of the federal policymaking process. Public policy Corrupting Wall Street means that small investors get fleeced by richer ones, like the insider trading millionaires who dominate Congress. That's bad for lots of people, from workers investing in 401k funds to retirees or people living on fixed incomes, who invest in stocks as part of a long-term plan for financial security, only to be preyed upon by powerful people using their elected offices as a lever to fill their wallets. But corrupting Congress is worse, both because it harms everyone (not only investors), and also because it neutralizes the source of potential support best poised to alleviate the vagaries of the market: democratically accountable government. The conflicts of interest across Congress hamstring public responses to corporate predation. They predictably encourage indefensible public policy outcomes--like, for instance, recurring alongside a federal minimum wage that has remained for well over a decade, or repeated approvals for military spending that inflate the profits of in which . This pattern is a crucial part of why President to fear the machinations of a military-industrial complex that has only tightened its grip around America's throat in the years since then. Elected officials Members of Congress who are more interested in capitalism than democracy are free to build their fortunes through private enterprise. Those who subject the machinery of government to the enlargement of their own fortunes display a disqualifying disregard for democracy by openly embracing conflicts of interest. Every one of them--including the senior leadership of both corporate parties in Washington--stands in open violation of a constitutional prohibition, despite swearing an oath to uphold that document and the principles it embodies. Editors & journalists As much as policymakers deserve criticism for failing to regulate themselves, another community deserves even greater blame for falling asleep at the switch: the newspaper editors whose primary responsibility was to expose corruption but instead chose to embrace it in plain sight. Congressional insider trading has never been OK. It's absurd that for the press to notice an obvious vector inviting conflicts of interest, and that even in 2023, few voices have acknowledged the profound institutional corruption resulting from it. It's equally absurd that this issue has fallen to the GOP only because editors have so pervasively failed to ethically cover political news in real-time. Observing the impacts of institutional media failure Hawley's proposal is neither the first to curtail congressional conflicts of interest, nor the most demanding. Two years before he introduced the PELOSI Act to curtail insider trading by policymakers, I ending the practice through a more aggressive solution that most voters never heard about. My proposal would have reached not only policymakers and their spouses, as Hawley's would, but also their children, extended family, and staff. No policy that leaves congressional family members or staff free to leverage inside information would suffice to stop conflicts of interest from subverting the policymaking process. Unlike Sen. Hawley, I also proposed to prohibit policymakers, their families and their staff from trading not only stocks and other financial instruments, but also from investing in private equity vehicles that are even more and unaccountable. Private equity has wrought remarkable havoc on markets, (even according to Wall Street ) and , as well as (coincidence?), and indeed, writ large. Any so-called "reform" that would leave in place opportunities for policymakers to launder their conflicts of interest by simply making them less visible--as Hawley's would--is more cosmetic than meaningful. We produced this advertisement supporting my 2022 congressional campaign to highlight the issue and summarize my proposal: Unlike most congressional races, our's did not suffer from a dearth of press coverage. Whether because we raised over $1.6 million, or instead because my voice attracted support from every state across the entire country and every county in California, around the country--from Rolling Stone and Salon to The Intercept and the Los Angeles Times--found our race newsworthy. Yet, in spite of all the attention, most voters never heard about my ideas or policy proposals. First, nearly every local broadcast outlet ignored us. Only two English-language local media covered us at all. KPFA, the local Pacifica Radio affiliate, covered our campaign the morning after I won the primary. And KTVU, the local Fox affiliate, posted in the waning weeks of the election. But aside from viewers of those two specific broadcasts, most San Franciscans had no idea that Pelosi was even up for re-election in 2020--let alone how the candidates differed, or that the November 2020 ballot included our city's first contested congressional election since 1987. But even among , the policies on which I ran--like banning congressional insider trading, or adopting universal healthcare during a global pandemic, or nationalizing the fossil fuel industry that continues to threaten humanity and life on Earth--appeared far less to editors than contrived by Democrats loyal to Pelosi. The corruption of policymakers enriching themselves, in other words, has been insulated by the corruption of writers and journalists who failed to ethically practice their profession and instead abandoned independence to publish partisan propaganda entrenching corruption. That's among the reasons why I continue to wage against the San Francisco Chronicle. Beyond recklessly and maliciously publishing racist disinformation fabricated to undermine a-once-in-a-generation-election, journalists from outlets including The Intercept , Mission Local , 48 Hills , the Bay Area Reporter , Current Affairs and others all enabled the continuing corruption of an intergenerational oligarch who continues to fleece the public while shamelessly filling her pockets. (Feel free to mark your calendar and join us for our next hearing before U.S. District Judge Chen, on February 2 around 1:30pm PDT, about which I'll share more details soon) In the same way that America continues to pay a price for Democrats who can't be bothered to show up for work, we have also been abandoned by newspaper editors whose abdication of independent reporting has to corrupt Democrats whose recurring failures in turn create opportunities for corrupt Republicans. We should all demand better, regardless of our ideology or political preferences. Ethics and corruption, like any constitutional issue, offer the opportunity to unite warring factions of voters around common sense principles. Stopping policymakers from profiting from their office is the very least we can do to start restoring faith and trust in government. Paid subscribers can access below a social media exchange from earlier this week in which I had an opportunity to correct a factually inaccurate statement by a Member of Congress about U.S. history and the first Black woman elected to Congress... Team —
On January 6, 2021, the very existence of our democracy was threatened and, for the first time in American history, the peaceful transition of power was interrupted.
The insurrectionists have not been defeated. The attempted coup on that day may have failed, but its architects have been emboldened and they are, in fact, planning the next one.
Today, we’re going to start fighting back. The Democracy Defense Fund is an organization committed to saving our country from the authoritarian traitors who seek to destroy it — but we need your help.
Will you join our pro-democracy movement by splitting a donation between the Democracy Defense Fund and Seth Moulton? Any amount helps in our fight to save democracy. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/moulton-ddf]
You know my uncle. He started the “Big Lie” and he continues to traffic in the right-wing extremism that puts our country at risk.
We must stop him and his loyal cronies and those, at all levels of government, who continue to undermine our democracy. That’s why I founded the Democracy Defense Fund — and it’s why Seth Moulton and I are asking for your help now.
We need resources to support organizations and people who are committed to protecting the right to vote and preserving our Constitution, just like Seth Moulton.
This includes an all-out effort to support campaigns at the local and state level in order to take back state houses and preserve election integrity.
All of that is necessary in order to defend our democracy, friend. And that’s what we’re committed to do here at Democracy Defense Fund.
This will only be possible if we all do our part — starting right now. So, will you split a donation between the Democracy Defense Fund and Seth Moulton today? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/moulton-ddf]
Thank you,
Mary

Rocky Mountain National Park is 108 years old today, but we need to act to protect it for the next 108! |

Today is the 108th birthday of our beloved Rocky Mountain National Park. [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/57847797/389263818/398564870?refcode=AU_MFB_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20230126_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjgvMS83NDc5MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5MDUwZDk1NS1jNzlkLWVkMTEtOTk0Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=WH_eZE4zsA0AEAoXjvAHSUVuwzBcrrQCy-IFhaxODCQ=) For more than a century, the beauty of Rocky Mountain has brought immeasurable joy to Colorado’s residents and visitors alike, but now Kevin McCarthy’s new far-right majority is putting our public lands in danger. As part of his concessions to become Speaker, McCarthy gave into the pressure of MAGA members like Lauren Boebert to put our public lands at risk. **McCarthy’s House majority created a loophole making it easier for public lands to be sold off.** [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/57847798/389263820/-1188936832?refcode=AU_MFB_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20230126_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjgvMS83NDc5MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5MDUwZDk1NS1jNzlkLWVkMTEtOTk0Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=WH_eZE4zsA0AEAoXjvAHSUVuwzBcrrQCy-IFhaxODCQ=) | [FIGHT BACK](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/57847801/389263821/-1188936832?refcode=AU_MFB_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20230126_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjgvMS83NDc5MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5MDUwZDk1NS1jNzlkLWVkMTEtOTk0Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=WH_eZE4zsA0AEAoXjvAHSUVuwzBcrrQCy-IFhaxODCQ=)

**Friend, Kevin McCarthy and the GOP will do anything to please their corporate cronies, even help give away our public lands.**
To fight back, we have to strengthen our Senate majority, support Colorado’s House Democrats, and flip vulnerable GOP seats (like Lauren Boebert’s) in 2024!

clean_body: Rocky Mountain National Park is 108 years old today, but we need to act to protect it for the next 108! | = Today is the 108th birthday of our beloved Rocky Mountain National Park. = For more than a century, the beauty of Rocky Mountain has brought immeasurable joy to Colorado’s residents and visitors alike, but now Kevin McCarthy’s new far-right majority is putting our public lands in danger. As part of his concessions to become Speaker, McCarthy gave into the pressure of MAGA members like Lauren Boebert to put our public lands at risk. McCarthy’s House majority created a loophole making it easier for public lands to be sold off. = | = Now, extractive industries like fossil fuel companies can gain cheap access to federal public lands — taking away the public lands we love so much, as well as the revenue to manage the parks, support workers, and create recreational areas. Friend, Kevin McCarthy and the GOP will do anything to please their corporate cronies, even help give away our public lands. To fight back, we have to strengthen our Senate majority, support Colorado’s House Democrats, and flip vulnerable GOP seats (like Lauren Boebert’s) in 2024! = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | = ** | = | | = | = | | = | = – Team Bennet Bennet for Colorado PO Box 3078 Denver, CO 80201 United States | mailto:info@michaelbennet.com | Paid for by Bennet for Colorado ** By clicking the links above, you certify that you are responsible for paying all charges incurred in using the debit or credit card to be charged, and that your personal funds will be the true source of the contribution.

Team, Believe it or not, January is already coming to a close, and with it comes our first fundraising deadline of 2023. **[Before I ask if you can chip in $10 or $25 toward our people-powered campaign](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/57824593/389224281/1850399781?refcode=fl_email_fr_20230126eom1_gv_od60ds_xx_l1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85MzAxMSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3YmUxMzU4Zi05ZjlkLWVkMTEtOTk0Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=9IZOpUqeRGN_iSVRG2m8JO7MCTPP_QlEGhaR8CyBqmY=)** , I hope you’ll let me explain why your contribution matters in this moment. You might be wondering why we’re already fundraising again, just two months after the 2022 election and three weeks since I was sworn-in to office. We used everything we fundraised last year to win this extremely competitive House race last November, with a margin of just 1,310 votes. _Every dollar you contributed, we spent to win and flip this toss-up seat blue._ **Now, my far-right opponent Yvette Herrell has already filed to run against me in 2024, and you can bet she’s spending 100% of her time fundraising.** I no longer have the same amount of time to commit to fundraising, since I’m focused on legislating and serving the people of NM-02 in Congress. But with our opponent already preparing for a 2024 rematch, we can’t afford to be caught flat-footed — we have to prepare, too! **[Our grassroots campaign exhausted our budget in 2022, so that’s why I’m asking: can you pitch in $10 or more right now to help us refuel and reach our fundraising goal before the January 31 end-of-month deadline?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/57824594/389224283/1850399781?refcode=fl_email_fr_20230126eom1_gv_od60ds_xx_l2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85MzAxMSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3YmUxMzU4Zi05ZjlkLWVkMTEtOTk0Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=9IZOpUqeRGN_iSVRG2m8JO7MCTPP_QlEGhaR8CyBqmY=)** | If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:

| | This sponsored message from Pro Life Update is on behalf of Hillsdale College [](http://e.prolifeupdate.com/rd/9z2zj3bpcgctqjob212ngsttvtvoch0ml2ki1k2scvo_rp22sh2s8i66pr1ctjm8pr0cjh2bs)
---
| | | Fellow American, Are K-12 schools, colleges, and universities in your state doing enough to provide students with a proper understanding of American history and government--including an understanding of the purposes and principles of the Constitution? There is reason to be doubtful. There seems to be a decreasing sense of patriotism among young Americans, and an increasing attraction to socialism and other ideas destructive of liberty. And have you noticed all the disturbing incidents taking place on college campuses, with students resorting to intimidation, threats, and violence to silence speakers with whom they disagree? Hillsdale is planning to redouble its efforts for the future of liberty. We will respond to these disturbing trends by expanding our educational outreach efforts on behalf of liberty--especially for younger Americans. And as we form our strategy we’d like to hear from you: [**Please take our brief education survey here »**](http://e.prolifeupdate.com/rd/9z2z0dmqnlnsg5325uhrv73h3hfvd6c4ufmrtqpgei0_rp22sh2s8i66pr1ctjm8pr0cjh2bs) We have done too many of our young people a disservice by failing to provide them with knowledge of our nation’s great heritage of liberty. Even worse, many of our schools, corrupted by Left-wing bias, have pushed a false history of America as essentially flawed and unjust. This indoctrination will only continue once students return to campuses across the country. Hillsdale can remain independent and stay true to its mission because it refuses to accept one penny of taxpayer funding--even indirect aid like federal or state student grants and loans. This frees Hillsdale from burdensome and corrupting regulation, but it also means we are entirely dependent on the support of our friends nationwide. Will you help us understand how Americans look at education today so that we can plan our expanded outreach in the smartest way possible? [**Please take a few minutes to complete this survey and let us know your opinions on education today »**](http://e.prolifeupdate.com/rd/9z2zgu8ps4s3uf7loc90rbsh7jitiihkt0iba1v512o_rp22sh2s8i66pr1ctjm8pr0cjh2bs) I am deeply grateful for your friendship.
---

Warm Regards,
Larry P. Arnn
President, Hillsdale College
Pursuing Truth - Defending Liberty since 1844
| | Hillsdale College 33 East College St Hillsdale, MI 49242 USA

---

body:

name: 4Patriots
subject: America's Biggest Threat In 2023
body:
BREAKING – A brutal attack on North Carolina's power grid has left 45,000 residents in the dark and scrambling for a way to st
# America's Biggest Threat In 2023
**BREAKING** - A brutal attack on North Carolina's power grid has left 45,000 residents in the dark and scrambling for a way to stay safe. In 2022 alone there have been over 70 attacks on the power grid with reports of six in Florida, five in Oregon and Washington. And according to national security experts, this trend is only going to continue. As America's power grid comes under increasing attack, will your city be the next to fall victim to this growing threat? It's a harsh reality, but one that we must face head on. **National security experts warn there is an imminent threat to the national electric grid.** In fact, attacks on just 9 of the nation's 55,000 electrical substations could result in nationwide blackouts for up to 18 months. Imagine a blackout lasting not days, but weeks or months. Your life would be frozen in time right at the moment the power fails. Lights all over the country would go out, throwing people into total darkness. Without access to a generator, your fridge, electric range and microwave would be dead. All the food in your fridge and freezer would spoil. Your well couldn't pump any fresh water into your house. And even if you don't have a well, a total grid failure means no fresh water to drink, cook or clean with. You wouldn't be able to operate your radio or TV or charge your cell phone, so you'd feel isolated and cut off from your friends and family. This is not some crazy doomsday theory, but a very real and imminent threat. Retired CENTCOM General Lloyd Austin has warned that it's not a question of if, but when. [**When massive blackouts strike the U.S.**](http://e.americanbriefing.com/rd/9z2z3rh5gbi1brlj7bt9iklesthebtcf942ne104sio_rp22sh2s8i66pr1ctjm4pr2chh2bs) Dr. Peter Pry, former CIA official and Chief of Staff of the Congressional EMP Commission, and other top national security experts have raised the alarm for years about the most vulnerable aspect of infrastructure in the United States: **Our crumbling electric grid.** When our electric grid fails, it will be like watching America have a heart attack right before your eyes, because when the heart stops pumping, everything shuts down and the patient flat lines. Our enemies could cripple our great country in a matter of minutes. Former CIA Director James Woolsey warns that an attack on America's infrastructure would, "blackout the national electric grid and other life-sustaining critical infrastructures for over a year killing 9 of 10 Americans by starvation and societal collapse." Our enemies could just sit back and watch as we self-destruct through looting, rioting and the targeting of police officers. **Our government is woefully unprepared.** It seems like the government has been fixated on taking more and more away from people who worked hard to earn it, while doing virtually nothing to secure our nation's infrastructure. They've ignored dire warnings from national security experts about the grid's vulnerability to physical, electro-magnetic pulse (EMP) and cyber-attacks. **"Our death toll would be staggering"** reports FOXNews. We are all fine when the power goes out for a few hours or even days. But an extended blackout would be devastating. Will you be prepared for a crisis that could take months or even years to recover from? How will you stay safe, warm and protected? [**What will you do when the power goes out?**](http://e.americanbriefing.com/rd/9z2z636svs35h561o8hjtqo2rrv6mbh5mv7mlti03go_rp22sh2s8i66pr1ctjm4pr2chh2bs) **That's why many Americans are taking matters into their own hands and are securing their own solar powered generator.** Solar generators are a smart choice because they produce an endless supply of life-saving electricity when you need it most - and **without** gas, fumes or noise. Our top recommendation in portable solar generators is the [**Patriot Power Generator**.](http://e.americanbriefing.com/rd/9z2zg4ngit8lc53cp045vp9s00jta440hrnosn2mjo0_rp22sh2s8i66pr1ctjm4pr2chh2bs) **It's a breakthrough NEW device that generates continuous watts of clean, quiet and FREE renewable power.** That's enough to power lights for safety and comfort, your computer, TV or cell phone. Even small appliances like a small freezer or critical medical device that will sustain your family during a power outage. [**You can see a live demo of it in action here**](http://e.americanbriefing.com/rd/9z2z9nthi643114eg79ic9s4s9965398ljuptq2vbu8_rp22sh2s8i66pr1ctjm4pr2chh2bs) Imagine how much peace of mind you'll have right away when you get your own. Because if a crisis hits and your family asks, "When will the power come back on?" you'll calmly reassure them that they're safe and they will have plenty of electricity to power the critical items. You'll be able to power lights, preserve food, recharge cell phones and computers or keep critical medical devices going. Your home will be a little island of light and warmth in a storm - be it natural or man-made. Listen, nobody can predict the future. None of us know exactly when or how the next attack will hit our electrical grid. But from everything we see, it could be devastating. Watch this video and find out for yourself... **[Click here to see it in action!](http://e.americanbriefing.com/rd/9z2zhmtod0l7hv1omki9l4s1k2b9ss4qbevf5rqc96o_rp22sh2s8i66pr1ctjm4pr2chh2bs)** To your survival,

Frank Bates
Patriot Headquarters
---

625 First Street #183, Alexandria, VA 22314

[](http://e.americanbriefing.com/rd/9z2zud79o02mcnihaqa7pd5m5lt1obbgl981gbafpa0_rp22sh2s8i66pr1ctjm4pr2chh238h5e8he49h666qbcdli6uon49fg)

name: Hendrell Remus
subject: fwd: The most corrupt person in the Senate
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Tennessee Democratic Party
body:
Peter,

Let me get straight to the point — it's unacceptable having Marsha Blackburn as our US Senator. She’s the epitome of Republican extremism and corruption because she only serves herself and her highest donors.

Marsha Blackburn:

❌ Has taken over $1 million from Big Pharma
❌ Made the opioid crisis worse
❌ Doesn’t believe women can make their own healthcare decisions

We need sustained, substantial investment in our year-round organizing program if we are going to have any shot at defeating her. Will you donate now to help us start building our war chest? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/defeatmarsha]

If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:

$5 » [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/defeatmarsha?express_lane=true&amount=5&refcode=express_lane_5]
$10 » [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/defeatmarsha?express_lane=true&amount=10&refcode=express_lane_10]
$25 » [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/defeatmarsha?express_lane=true&amount=25&refcode=express_lane_25]
$50 » [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/defeatmarsha?express_lane=true&amount=50&refcode=express_lane_50]
$100 » [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/defeatmarsha?express_lane=true&amount=100&refcode=express_lane_100]
OTHER » [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/defeatmarsha?express_lane_other=true&refcode=express_lane_other]

Everything is at stake. We absolutely have to remove Marsha Blackburn from office in 2024 but to do that we need to start building now. As I’m writing to you, she’s doing everything she can to spread hate and conspiracy theories while tripling down on dividing us. She has no limits on who she will attack or what absurd thing she’ll say to please the extremists on the other side.

Building back Democratic infrastructure takes time, which is why we are in this for the long haul. Each and every one of you has played a crucial role in our success so far, and we're not stopping now.

Supporting the Tennessee Democratic Party has never been more important. Chip in $10 now to help us start off our year strong [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/defeatmarsha] [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/defeatmarsha] → [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tndemocraticparty]

We’re the only ones who can save ourselves from Republicans like Marsha Blackburn. Tennesseans from every corner of our state must come together to say enough. Are you as tired as we are of having someone so embarrassing representing us? We can do better but the truth is, we can't do this without you.

We're in this together,

Hendrell Remus
he/him
Chair, Tennessee Democratic Party

name: Dan Kovats

body:
What can you do to help?

[](https://act.myngp.com/el/KFMUICwTFSI7Fk03dOTB_gURNakmgkg0kxuKssc0-5Q=/adQiPfAnd9UwN0wP7IeCVKKntgcScPzh8MAKB8QYAQ4=?refcode=EM_FR_Welcome2_DK_NTL_6_NG_N_Logo)
Fellow Democrats,

The Illinois Democratic County Chairs' Association was formed over 50 years ago -- and folks rely on us to help elect Democrats across the state.

If there is one principle that we live by, it's that every Democrat, everywhere counts.

Our mission is to build a dynamic and sustainable resource organization for Democrats across Illinois. That's why we provide training and best practices guidance to all 102 Illinois Democratic County Parties and their members.

**[Fellow Democrats, candidate training and all of the services we offer to Democrats are vital, but they also cost money. As an organization that works all year, every year, every dollar we make in contributions from grassroots donors like you matters. Will you chip in $10 today to help us make sure our important work continues?](https://act.myngp.com/el/KFMUICwTFSI7Fk03dOTB_gURNakmgkg0kxuKssc0-5Q=/adQiPfAnd9UwN0wP7IeCVKKntgcScPzh8MAKB8QYAQ4=?refcode=EM_FR_Welcome2_DK_NTL_6_NG_N_L1&amount=10)**
[**DONATE NOW**](https://act.myngp.com/el/KFMUICwTFSI7Fk03dOTB_gURNakmgkg0kxuKssc0-5Q=/adQiPfAnd9UwN0wP7IeCVKKntgcScPzh8MAKB8QYAQ4=?refcode=EM_FR_Welcome2_DK_NTL_6_NG_N_B1&amount=10) The IDCCA is committed to Democratic values and victories at every level, including at the county, statewide, and national levels. As the largest and most active grassroots Democratic partner in Illinois, we focus on being a resource for the Democratic County Parties.

**[We all benefit when Democrats have the resources they need to win. And those resources are expensive to build. Can you contribute anything now to help us ensure every Democrat across our state gets the resources and support they need?](https://act.myngp.com/el/KFMUICwTFSI7Fk03dOTB_gURNakmgkg0kxuKssc0-5Q=/adQiPfAnd9UwN0wP7IeCVKKntgcScPzh8MAKB8QYAQ4=?refcode=EM_FR_Welcome2_DK_NTL_6_NG_N_L2)**

name: Josh Harder
subject: a big decision coming up
body:
Last year, you helped us staff up incredibly early so we could build an unbeatable ground game to protect our toss-up district. And the strategy worked.
Hi Peter,
Last year, you helped us staff up incredibly early so we could build an unbeatable ground game to protect our toss-up district. And the strategy worked. Our team built a truly grassroots organization and knocked on tens of thousands of voters’ doors.
Now going into 2024, we know Trump will be on the ballot again and Republicans are even more energized. We have to win this seat to take back the Speaker’s gavel from Kevin McCarthy and Marjorie Taylor Greene.
In order to win, we need to get started early. That’s why at the end of this month, I’m going to be making a big decision. How many campaign staff are we able to bring on for the start of the 2023-2024 election cycle?
It’s all going to depend on how much we can raise by January 31st. So will you please send in a generous donation right now to help us reach our $10,000 end-of-month goal?
I’ll leave my personal link here: [**https://secure.actblue.com/donate/JHCA-09-Email-Jan2023-FR-EOM**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/57848590/389266415/-1990234082?refcode=E20115-20230126-EOMPersonal-SL-DeadlineEOM-RegTemplate-NA-NA&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjQvMS8xMDMxMTgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiZDkxOTM4NjQtY2M5ZC1lZDExLTk5NGMtMDAyMjQ4MzJlYjczIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=o4KosLsf_0yF9ftzb1abHUisGtXPBpSzTpXbg8llGLU=)
Thanks for helping us fight back when it matters the most!
\- Josh

Josh Harder for Congress
10 N California St
Stockton, CA 95202
United States

| | Peter, this week, we celebrate National School Choice and the opportunities it has provided to families across the country. Unfortunately, school choice opponents are actively trying to undo our hard work and rip away choices from parents and children. They wish to roll back current programs and force children back into failing schools. **We can’t allow that to happen**.
**Peter, we’re asking for your support to help us fight back by pledging a**[ _**$10**_](http://links.newsletter.conservativedirect.com/u/click?_t=5b1853ae48754bee8f471363fe4933a9&_m=acec1d4adc414ddba8fe38d3bfc2df1c&_e=sW3O-aJVoQOaGkpcDYvM5h5Xm85PFT71BgeKeaD5c8JpikZngloBXU8FuZyxCqdBqqJJuqh2NuwGrwGhROrHFg7DODy4kUkhxpNJAzeSu7dEA0VGvb3xCAYPcqxholohn3B_4srwG7xwTiZdlfhTOTnyeJEkZrQBkjJ6dYukQyJ58WhjaZk9jCcwx2Arn3yZvMmEHQia0p3M2KlBoZd1gbQ9fbVynK2Jq2nWHfqmlpoijpPFGUT4pzGJe2ZOx3wE1ePQPXahvesWt4JFjy6cq3iWVfIvwV4GPItghS_9uldcAI2EQZDK6iSfa09C3-mqCekYmwFaRNKXXE6udR-_twNC5gOCzaq5s9I7-2FaGLdQShAQW5ibTIuAiNtlp9Upzc5RVee-43Hmsa1abdQlRdy8h7N8ErdyYaL4-Ia8sz_4YkQHSw3-7OTKFplFjG-r)**,**[_**$25**_](http://links.newsletter.conservativedirect.com/u/click?_t=5b1853ae48754bee8f471363fe4933a9&_m=acec1d4adc414ddba8fe38d3bfc2df1c&_e=sW3O-aJVoQOaGkpcDYvM5h5Xm85PFT71BgeKeaD5c8JpikZngloBXU8FuZyxCqdBqqJJuqh2NuwGrwGhROrHFg7DODy4kUkhxpNJAzeSu7cPyOAFNVO6pqOfQev-XAZHdJlqBQ8_cbXQVC0lAhm8NtvF43aevom0_oFslh2PdsASh9r7Ffa853K3aRpTy3TQZWpiVlAWorhVcCXOwjgTdgTvVhc96V7l1rPyiqYoHbJB68wibi4d3hzotswaMTVQ4oN7et098YHyleviC6lu9LIfVMkwsyxX_efBojwbQ5J1pTsYe7j1TSZHGkU62JyO--6RMK-9XMimXguyegZ1s6ehrnsSq4qlQa-3qG_lEkUJh5mCkdL20Uk-RWbgJW7iG1wVN31oYOyHJha3VJ4E1JM9g2wwo9T0SlnTsYEXW48pB78LhdQd6Rak90eiixLI)**, or even**[ _**$50**_](http://links.newsletter.conservativedirect.com/u/click?_t=5b1853ae48754bee8f471363fe4933a9&_m=acec1d4adc414ddba8fe38d3bfc2df1c&_e=sW3O-aJVoQOaGkpcDYvM5h5Xm85PFT71BgeKeaD5c8JpikZngloBXU8FuZyxCqdBqqJJuqh2NuwGrwGhROrHFg7DODy4kUkhxpNJAzeSu7d2adkhV1MZR3q27ataA167lPMZ-pkfaIFl0lY_XmMwF9xQMIIIVeGikjZFSQcUrAM31RZg5Z50d6k-7JDNXGplZa7X8stDSjmDeNW5qFUpJnFc5F_ATdSBgWuEs7jDekj5l3SRuyvWAaP-nZgwIdyYQVfuqKnGc2Uz-ugE9F3MKf0iJgNUJMYh-00HGWZYeJ9bQ37DYeuJAAz8tET5g0hLz2lftVw_bDLydgBF5w03P198_TuiAGZIz1wEK0Or4pSTtSiRYcwcxwts_2x7IHD_eLpG-PICCIGaSGrkOegIH8SNQhrZfjvlkqvC7CZmFDiV6D1utfU4hyr0goxs4Xbp)**donation during National School Choice Week.****_Can we count you in?_**

name: 4Patriots
subject: America's Biggest Threat In 2023
body:
BREAKING – A brutal attack on North Carolina's power grid has left 45,000 residents in the dark and scrambling for a way to st
# America's Biggest Threat In 2023
**BREAKING** - A brutal attack on North Carolina's power grid has left 45,000 residents in the dark and scrambling for a way to stay safe. In 2022 alone there have been over 70 attacks on the power grid with reports of six in Florida, five in Oregon and Washington. And according to national security experts, this trend is only going to continue. As America's power grid comes under increasing attack, will your city be the next to fall victim to this growing threat? It's a harsh reality, but one that we must face head on. **National security experts warn there is an imminent threat to the national electric grid.** In fact, attacks on just 9 of the nation's 55,000 electrical substations could result in nationwide blackouts for up to 18 months. Imagine a blackout lasting not days, but weeks or months. Your life would be frozen in time right at the moment the power fails. Lights all over the country would go out, throwing people into total darkness. Without access to a generator, your fridge, electric range and microwave would be dead. All the food in your fridge and freezer would spoil. Your well couldn't pump any fresh water into your house. And even if you don't have a well, a total grid failure means no fresh water to drink, cook or clean with. You wouldn't be able to operate your radio or TV or charge your cell phone, so you'd feel isolated and cut off from your friends and family. This is not some crazy doomsday theory, but a very real and imminent threat. Retired CENTCOM General Lloyd Austin has warned that it's not a question of if, but when. [**When massive blackouts strike the U.S.**](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zhtplfcjhhfu41k1n3138h7mlm7gpo6s7ghce380_rp22sh2s8i66pr1ctjmco70s5h2bs) Dr. Peter Pry, former CIA official and Chief of Staff of the Congressional EMP Commission, and other top national security experts have raised the alarm for years about the most vulnerable aspect of infrastructure in the United States: **Our crumbling electric grid.** When our electric grid fails, it will be like watching America have a heart attack right before your eyes, because when the heart stops pumping, everything shuts down and the patient flat lines. Our enemies could cripple our great country in a matter of minutes. Former CIA Director James Woolsey warns that an attack on America's infrastructure would, "blackout the national electric grid and other life-sustaining critical infrastructures for over a year killing 9 of 10 Americans by starvation and societal collapse." Our enemies could just sit back and watch as we self-destruct through looting, rioting and the targeting of police officers. **Our government is woefully unprepared.** It seems like the government has been fixated on taking more and more away from people who worked hard to earn it, while doing virtually nothing to secure our nation's infrastructure. They've ignored dire warnings from national security experts about the grid's vulnerability to physical, electro-magnetic pulse (EMP) and cyber-attacks. **"Our death toll would be staggering"** reports FOXNews. We are all fine when the power goes out for a few hours or even days. But an extended blackout would be devastating. Will you be prepared for a crisis that could take months or even years to recover from? How will you stay safe, warm and protected? [**What will you do when the power goes out?**](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zkje6c4rirvmo7i8bpr3lliro09o5tn58q3aj1p8_rp22sh2s8i66pr1ctjmco70s5h2bs) **That's why many Americans are taking matters into their own hands and are securing their own solar powered generator.** Solar generators are a smart choice because they produce an endless supply of life-saving electricity when you need it most - and **without** gas, fumes or noise. Our top recommendation in portable solar generators is the [**Patriot Power Generator**.](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z47menoth7gvu6c33ouuqtluuls0u348e3hmtji0_rp22sh2s8i66pr1ctjmco70s5h2bs) **It's a breakthrough NEW device that generates continuous watts of clean, quiet and FREE renewable power.** That's enough to power lights for safety and comfort, your computer, TV or cell phone. Even small appliances like a small freezer or critical medical device that will sustain your family during a power outage. [**You can see a live demo of it in action here**](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zhid1dt8vsth2pb5iqf796q2tt8ohkjkk3qf6gbg_rp22sh2s8i66pr1ctjmco70s5h2bs) Imagine how much peace of mind you'll have right away when you get your own. Because if a crisis hits and your family asks, "When will the power come back on?" you'll calmly reassure them that they're safe and they will have plenty of electricity to power the critical items. You'll be able to power lights, preserve food, recharge cell phones and computers or keep critical medical devices going. Your home will be a little island of light and warmth in a storm - be it natural or man-made. Listen, nobody can predict the future. None of us know exactly when or how the next attack will hit our electrical grid. But from everything we see, it could be devastating. Watch this video and find out for yourself... **[Click here to see it in action!](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z4rd5j6m1igr89b7fk47lcb0krpbtnkvo75dpetg_rp22sh2s8i66pr1ctjmco70s5h2bs)** To your survival,

Frank Bates
Patriot Headquarters
---

625 First Street #183, Alexandria, VA 22314

name: Vice President Kamala Harris
subject: President Biden and I aren’t backing down, and we hope you won’t either.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Democratic National Committee, **[www
body:
This is a moment for us all to stand together to protect the freedom and liberty of all people, all women everywhere. | |
---
Derek -- For nearly 50 years, Americans relied on the rights that _Roe v. Wade_ protected -- the fundamental constitutional right of a woman to make decisions about her own body. This month, however, on what would have been its 50th anniversary, we speak of the _Roe_ decision in the past tense. The Supreme Court’s action to take away that constitutional right has meant that many dedicated doctors and nurses now lose their ability to care for their patients, that providers risk going to jail just for doing their job, and that patients are denied critical care and even fear that they will be punished simply for seeking care. We are fighting back. President Biden has directed our cabinet to identify barriers to accessing prescription medication and to recommend actions to make sure that doctors can legally prescribe, pharmacies can dispense, and women can secure safe and effective medication. Still, many states have gone even further and now have total bans in effect. _That is why I am asking you for support._ **Make a $7 donation to the DNC today to support the organizing, voter protection, and other critical programs that will help elect Democrats nationwide.**

clean_body: This is a moment for us all to stand together to protect the freedom and liberty of all people, all women everywhere. | | Derek -- For nearly 50 years, Americans relied on the rights that Roe v. Wade protected -- the fundamental constitutional right of a woman to make decisions about her own body. This month, however, on what would have been its 50th anniversary, we speak of the Roe decision in the past tense. The Supreme Court’s action to take away that constitutional right has meant that many dedicated doctors and nurses now lose their ability to care for their patients, that providers risk going to jail just for doing their job, and that patients are denied critical care and even fear that they will be punished simply for seeking care. We are fighting back. President Biden has directed our cabinet to identify barriers to accessing prescription medication and to recommend actions to make sure that doctors can legally prescribe, pharmacies can dispense, and women can secure safe and effective medication. Still, many states have gone even further and now have total bans in effect. That is why I am asking you for support. Make a $7 donation to the DNC today to support the organizing, voter protection, and other critical programs that will help elect Democrats nationwide. Since our founding, we have been on a march forward to fully realize our promise to complete the unfinished work to secure freedom and liberty for all. But, Derek, these outcomes will not be inevitable. It takes steadfast determination and dedication. The kind of determination and dedication possessed by some of our greatest patriots: Those Americans who fought a Civil War to end the sin of slavery, who organized at Seneca Falls to secure a woman’s right to vote, who launched the Freedom Rides to advance civil rights; and who spoke out at the Stonewall Inn to defend human rights. And we know this fight will not be won until we secure these rights for every American. President Biden and I will never back down, and we hope to have you by our side in this fight. Thank you for all that you do, Kamala Kamala Harris Vice President of the United States Kamala Harris: Derivative of Adam Schultz / Biden for President Photo. subject: I agree with Trump (seriously)

body:
Peter, I can’t believe what I’m hearing. Kevin McCarthy promised the far-right Freedom Caucus that he’d cut Medicare and Social Security if they voted for him as Speaker. Peter \-- even Donald Trump just came out against these Medicare and Social Security cuts. name: Katie Porter
subject: Not a dime from corporate PACs
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Katie Porter for Senate
body:
Our Senator should work for Californians, not corporations.
# [](https://act.katieporter.com/go/14558?t=1&akid=16580%2E3422442%2Ecvr8lW)
Derek,
**New: In the race for California’s U.S. Senate seat, I'm proud to be the only candidate who has never taken a dime of corporate PAC money.**
* I’ve said no to money from **Big Oil** companies who price gouge families and pollute our coastline.
* I’ve said no to money from **Big Pharma** companies who rip off patients and keep Americans sick.
* I’ve said no to money from **payday lenders** who profit off of a broken economic system.
* I’ve said no to money from **Wall Street Banks** who I’ve spent my career standing up to.

**And I’m proud to be one of the few Members of Congress who rejects federal lobbyist money.**
If people want to meet with me, they can bring their good ideas, not their checkbooks. Washington isn’t working for working people, and I’m running for Senate to not just talk about that, but to actually stand up to special interests and multinational corporations to make life better for California families.
I’m so proud to be running a grassroots powered campaign, and I need your help. **[Can you please make a contribution of $36 (that’s our average contribution!) today?](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kp4s-em-20230126-1?amount=36&refcode=EMAF26_1_ANN_20230126_V1_F&refcode2=16580_3422442_cvr8lW&akid=16580%2E3422442%2Ecvr8lW)**
If you've stored your info with ActBlue Express, we'll process your contribution instantly:
[Donate $15 ›››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kp4s-em-20230126-1?express_lane=true&amount=15&refcode=EMAF26_1_ANN_20230126_V1_F-el&refcode2=16580_3422442_cvr8lW&akid=16580%2E3422442%2Ecvr8lW) [Donate $36 ›››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kp4s-em-20230126-1?express_lane=true&amount=36&refcode=EMAF26_1_ANN_20230126_V1_F-el&refcode2=16580_3422442_cvr8lW&akid=16580%2E3422442%2Ecvr8lW) [Donate $50 ›››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kp4s-em-20230126-1?express_lane=true&amount=50&refcode=EMAF26_1_ANN_20230126_V1_F-el&refcode2=16580_3422442_cvr8lW&akid=16580%2E3422442%2Ecvr8lW) [Donate $100 ›››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kp4s-em-20230126-1?express_lane=true&amount=100&refcode=EMAF26_1_ANN_20230126_V1_F-el&refcode2=16580_3422442_cvr8lW&akid=16580%2E3422442%2Ecvr8lW) [Donate $250 ›››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kp4s-em-20230126-1?express_lane=true&amount=250&refcode=EMAF26_1_ANN_20230126_V1_F-el&refcode2=16580_3422442_cvr8lW&akid=16580%2E3422442%2Ecvr8lW) [Other amount ›››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kp4s-em-20230126-1?refcode=EMAF26_1_ANN_20230126_V1_F&refcode2=16580_3422442_cvr8lW&akid=16580%2E3422442%2Ecvr8lW)

This will be a tough and ultra-competitive race, and I’d be so honored to have you on our team.
Katie Porter

Katie is running for the U.S. Senate because Californians deserve a warrior fighting for them in Washington.
